one_line_takeaway: SIF distills decoding-phase text watermarks into a small imperceptible perturbation of the input image (via SAFD) and hardens it against representation drift from quantization/fine-tuning (via RFO), yielding LVLM ownership fingerprints that are simultaneously far stealthier against the paper's own Semantic Divergence Attack (perplexity 44.56 vs. 1261-2467 for prior methods) and more robust (higher FMR in every quantization/fine-tuning setting tested) than prior LVLM fingerprinting baselines (IF, Proflingo, PLA).
key_finding: SIF outperforms all baselines on FMR across every setting tested. On LLaVA-1.5-7B: 4-bit/8-bit quantization FMR of 0.49/0.89 (best baseline PLA: 0.40/0.79); full fine-tuning on LlavaMix/TikZ 0.31/0.37 vs PLA 0.00/0.13; in-house fine-tuning on MathV/TextVQA/V7W/Paintingform 0.49/0.59/0.52/0.67. On Qwen2.5-VL-7B: 4-bit/8-bit 0.88/0.98 vs PLA 0.76/0.94; GUI-Actor/ARC-AGI-1 fine-tuning 0.72/0.89; MathV/TextVQA/V7W/Paintingform 0.45/0.43/0.71/0.47. Under the Semantic Divergence Attack, SIF retains FMR of 0.86 (LLaVA) and 0.85 (Qwen) while Proflingo and IF drop to 0 and PLA drops to 0.07/0.11 (Table 3); trigger-query perplexity for SIF averages 44.56 versus 2467.4 for IF and 1261.3 for Proflingo, close to the natural-query baseline of 75.7 (Table 1). Under the stronger GPT-4.1 semantic judge, only 4% of SIF interactions are flagged as suspicious versus 86% for PLA. Reliability: FMR is 0 on all unrelated cross-family models for both SIF and PLA (Table 5), so the accuracy gain does not come at the cost of false positives. Ablations show SAFD alone already beats PLA, and RFO with rho=0.5 gives the largest additional robustness gain (e.g., 0.31 FMR on LlavaMix, 0.49 on MathV, versus w/o-RFO 0.26/0.42). Input-side robustness is a weak point: under JPEG compression and resizing, SIF's FMR drops to 0.18/0.23 (versus 0.84/0.86 for paraphrasing/sampling on the output side), and while this is still non-zero, PLA collapses to exactly 0 under the same input perturbations (Table 4). Generating one fingerprint query takes about 6 minutes on a single H100 GPU (one-time offline cost).
core_contribution: The paper's own contribution is two-fold. (1) Semantic Divergence Attack (SDA): a novel attack that reveals existing LVLM fingerprinting/ownership-verification schemes (Instructional Fingerprint/IF, Proflingo, Parameter Learning Attack/PLA) rely on semantically abnormal trigger queries or out-of-distribution responses, and that these can be detected/filtered by comparing perplexity and lexical/semantic divergence of the stolen model's output against a lightweight reference LVLM (InternVL3-1B), then substituting the reference model's response. (2) SIF (Semantically In-Distribution Fingerprints): a non-intrusive, black-box, parameter-free ownership verification framework consisting of Semantic-Aligned Fingerprint Distillation (SAFD), which distills a decoding-phase text watermark (unigram watermarking scheme of Zhao et al.) into a small L-infinity-bounded perturbation of an input image so that the frozen LVLM naturally produces semantically coherent responses containing a detectable watermark signal, and Robust-Fingerprint Optimization (RFO), which simulates worst-case norm-bounded activation perturbations during optimization (gradient-aligned perturbation added to intermediate hidden states) to make the fingerprint resilient to post-hoc model modification such as quantization and fine-tuning.
threat_model: Stealer: has full white-box access to a released LVLM (parameters), deploys it as a paid/proprietary API, and can modify it via quantization or fine-tuning to evade fingerprint checks; also may run a semantic-divergence filter (SDA) using a lightweight reference VLM to detect and strip fingerprint interactions before they reach the true stolen model. Developer: wants to verify whether a suspect black-box API originates from their released LVLM, has only black-box input-output access to the suspect model, and does not know how the stealer modified/deployed it. Design goals stated by the authors: effectiveness, reliability (no false positives on unrelated models), stealthiness (fingerprint queries/responses must look like normal semantically coherent QA), and robustness (survive quantization/fine-tuning).
limitations: The paper is explicit that: (1) FMR under JPEG compression and image resizing is markedly lower (0.18-0.23) than under output-side variations (0.78-0.86), i.e., the image-embedded fingerprint is comparatively fragile to input-side image processing even though it still beats PLA's total failure (0) there. (2) The watermark scheme underlying SAFD (unigram, logit-offset watermarking) is the same class of decoding-based text watermark that has documented removal attacks in the literature (cited but not evaluated against in this paper) -- the authors argue informally in Section 6.2 that removal is 'unrealistic' given the limited number of secret, per-query-varied fingerprint queries mixed with normal traffic, but this claim is not empirically tested against an adaptive watermark-removal adversary. (3) Fingerprint generation requires white-box (gradient) access to the LVLM being fingerprinted at construction time, i.e., only the developer who holds the original model can create SIF fingerprints; it does not, and does not claim to, allow post-hoc fingerprinting of already-deployed third-party models. (4) Evaluation is limited to two 7B-scale open LVLM families (LLaVA-1.5, Qwen2.5-VL); the paper does not test larger models, though it discusses the SDA attack scenario in the context of a stolen model potentially being 72B+ parameters. (5) The GPT-4.1 semantic-judge stealthiness result (4% vs 86% flagged) is stated to have full details 'in Supplementary,' which was not fully captured in the paper reviewed here.

datasets_used: ImageNet-2012 validation set (trigger image source, 200 sampled images)
AMBER benchmark (source of generation-task prompts for fingerprint queries)
VisionArena (used to measure SDA false-positive rate on normal user queries)
LlavaMix (259k vision-language instruction-following interactions; off-the-shelf fine-tuning eval)
TikZ (~10k image-code pairs; off-the-shelf fine-tuning eval)
GUI-Actor (~1M GUI screenshots / 10M annotated UI elements; off-the-shelf fine-tuning eval)
quantitative_results: LLaVA-1.5-7B FMR: 4-bit quant 0.49, 8-bit quant 0.89 (SIF) vs PLA 0.40/0.79 (Table 2)
LLaVA-1.5-7B full fine-tuning FMR: LlavaMix 0.31, TikZ 0.37, MathV 0.49, TextVQA 0.59, V7W 0.52, Paintingform 0.67 (SIF, Table 2)
Qwen2.5-VL-7B FMR: 4-bit quant 0.88, 8-bit quant 0.98 (SIF) vs PLA 0.76/0.94 (Table 2)
Qwen2.5-VL-7B full fine-tuning FMR: GUI-Actor 0.72, ARC-AGI-1 0.89, MathV 0.45, TextVQA 0.43, V7W 0.71, Paintingform 0.47 (SIF, Table 2)
FMR under Semantic Divergence Attack (Table 3): SIF 0.86 (LLaVA) / 0.85 (Qwen) vs Proflingo 0 / 0, IF 0 / 0, PLA 0.07 / 0.11
Trigger query perplexity (Table 1): Normal queries avg 75.7 (min 10.2, max 804.5); IF avg 2467.4; Proflingo avg 1261.3; PLA avg 46.67; SIF avg 44.56 (min 30.52, max 53.52)
GPT-4.1 semantic-judge flag rate: SIF 4% vs PLA 86%
Output/input robustness (Table 4, LLaVA-1.5-7B): SIF FMR under paraphrasing 0.78, sampling 0.80, JPEG 0.18, resize 0.23; PLA 0.84/0.86/0/0
Reliability on unrelated models (Table 5): FMR = 0 for both SIF and PLA across LLaVA-1.5-13B, LLaVA-mistral, Qwen2.5-VL-3B/7B, MiniGPT-4
RFO ablation (Table 6, LLaVA-1.5-7B): PLA baseline Int4/LlavaMix/TikZ/MathV = 0.40/0.00/0.13/0.37; SAFD w/o RFO = 0.46/0.26/0.28/0.42; RFO rho=0.5 = 0.49/0.31/0.37/0.49 (best)
SDA computational overhead: InternVL3-1B reference model adds 14.23% (relative to LLaVA-1.5-7B) and 14.48% (relative to Qwen2.5-VL-7B) compute overhead, but negligible latency since run in parallel
SDA false-positive rate on normal VisionArena queries: less than 5%
Fingerprint generation cost: ~6 minutes per query on a single NVIDIA H100 GPU (one-time offline)
baselines_compared: IF / Instructional Fingerprint (instruction-tuning backdoor fingerprint, Xu et al.)
Proflingo (adversarial prompt-prefix fingerprinting for LLMs, Jin et al.)
PLA / Parameter Learning Attack (adversarial trigger images with parameter perturbation, Wang et al.)
Ordinary attack and RNA (PLA variants; RNA adds parameter noise to simulate fine-tuning)
DIM (input-diversity adversarial example transferability method, repurposed as fingerprint baseline)
CroPA (random-cropping-based adversarial transferability method, repurposed as fingerprint baseline)
